_setup: Local WordPress MCP uses STDIO via WP-CLI as the user `loamkit-mcp` — NO application password, NO secrets in this file. The default above is the DDEV/Docker form; `scripts/scaffold.sh` picks the runner from resolved-config.json (Docker keeps `command:"ddev"`; for a no-Docker project it rewrites the wordpress server to {"command":"wp","args":["mcp-adapter","serve","--server=mcp-adapter-default-server","--user=loamkit-mcp"]}) when it writes ./.mcp.json — no manual edit needed. The mcp-adapter plugin install + the least-privilege `loamkit-mcp` user are provisioned automatically by `scripts/setup-mcp.sh` (requires WP >=6.9 / Abilities API, PHP >=7.4). For a REMOTE/production site, do NOT use STDIO — use the HTTP-proxy + Application Password variant instead (@automattic/mcp-wordpress-remote with WP_API_URL/USERNAME/PASSWORD), documented in reference/docker.md and reference/no-docker.md. The autonomous agent works against LOCAL only, never prod.
